paper.title: Agentic Entropy-Balanced Policy Optimization
paper.authors: Guanting Dong, Licheng Bao, Zhongyuan Wang, Kangzhi Zhao, Xiaoxi Li, Jiajie Jin, Jinghan Yang, Hangyu Mao, Fuzheng Zhang, Kun Gai, Guorui Zhou, Yutao Zhu, Ji-Rong Wen, Zhicheng Dou

entries.snippet: AEPO comprises dynamic entropy-balanced rollout and Entropy-Balanced Policy Optimization; 14 datasets; Qwen3-14B 47.6 GAIA, 11.2 HLE, 43.0 WebWalkerQA Pass@1; 65.0 GAIA, 26.0 HLE, 70.0 WebWalkerQA Pass@5.
SFT struggles to discover autonomous tool-use; trajectory-level RL overlooks multi-turn tool interactions; entropy-driven branching introduces training challenges.
Consecutive high-entropy tool-call turns: 56.5% consecutive vs 43.5% isolated; up to 6 consecutive; 93.4% branches concentrated on 1-3 trajectories.
High-entropy tokens include logical transitions and tool-call tokens; vanilla RL clips high-entropy token gradients, causing lack of gradient support early in training.
Tools: web search engine, web browser, code executor.
AEPO has dynamic entropy-balanced rollout and entropy-balanced policy optimization.
Allocate global vs branch rollout budget based on H_root - H_tool_avg through a sigmoid-controlled rule.
Monitor entropy variation after tool calls; penalize consecutive high-entropy branching with probability decreasing as consecutive branch count grows.
Insert stop-gradient into high-entropy clipping term to retain/rescale gradients for high-entropy tokens; add entropy-aware advantage estimation.
VERL; batch size 128; PPO mini-batch 16; context length 20K; global rollout 16; beta=0.2; P(l)=0.2*l; 16 H800 GPUs; Bing Search API.
Deep information seeking: Qwen3-14B + AEPO reaches WebWalkerQA avg 43.0, HLE 11.2, GAIA 47.6; Qwen3-8B + AEPO improves over ARPO.
Across 10 reasoning tasks: AEPO avg 56.3 on Llama3.1-8B and 60.3 on Qwen2.5-7B, highest among compared RL algorithms.
Qwen3-14B + AEPO Pass@5: GAIA 65%, HLE 26%, XBench-DR 65%; stable gains over ARPO.
AEPO forms more cluster centers than ARPO, 62 vs 54, and covers more branch paths.
AEPO uses about half the tool calls of vanilla/clipping-optimized RL algorithms while maintaining stable entropy curves and better accuracy.

entries.caption: Two high-entropy challenges: rollout collapse and token gradient clipping.
Quantitative statistics of the two entropy-based challenges.
AEPO overview: entropy-balanced rollout and policy update.
Pass@1 to Pass@5 comparison between ARPO and AEPO.
Branch sampling distribution and tool-call efficiency.
Training dynamics: entropy loss and accuracy.
